content:
The Panel as a Unit of Time
• In film, time is controlled by editing and pacing.
• In prose, time is controlled by sentence structure and paragraph breaks.
• In comics, time is controlled by the size, shape, and placement of panels on a page.

🔥 Example from Understanding Comics
McCloud shows a single moment stretched across multiple panels to slow time down. A character reaching for a glass can take five panels, making it feel painfully slow and suspenseful .

🔥 Example from Maus (Art Spiegelman)
• When Vladek tells his Holocaust survival story, the panels shift in size and layout to reflect his fragmented memories.
• McCloud argues that panel design influences how we perceive narrative time—a technique film and prose cannot replicate in the same way.

📌 For Your Graphic Novel:
• A wide, cinematic panel feels like a long, slow moment.
• Tight, small panels create rapid, intense pacing.
context: From document: 1.1.2. | Between the Frames | How Storytelling Transcends Mediums — chunk 2 (chunk 2/9)
